title: Fixed "subscription_period" docstring
body:
## Description
Fixed method docstring for "subscription_period"

Python version: 3.10

OS: macOS Sonoma 14.7

## Checklist:
- Just minor change of param docstring

step_plan.summary: Fixes the user-facing docstrings for the `subscription_period` parameter on the sync (`TeleBot`) and async (`AsyncTeleBot`) `create_chat_subscription_invite_link` methods, aligning documentation with Telegram’s Bot API while leaving runtime behavior unchanged.

steps.operation: Locate and correct the `subscription_period` parameter description in the synchronous wrapper method docstring (keeping ReST/Sphinx formatting intact and not touching runtime logic).
steps.reason: This public API wrapper is the primary user-facing documentation surface; fixing the docstring here ensures `subscription_period` is accurately documented for users calling the sync bot method.
steps.side_effects: No behavioral changes expected; only documentation output (help(), generated docs) will change. Ensure the corrected wording still matches the actual payload key (`subscription_period`) and does not imply different units/ranges than Telegram Bot API. If the project has docstring linting/doc build checks, they may need to be rerun.

steps.operation: Mirror the same `subscription_period` docstring correction in the async wrapper method so sync/async APIs remain consistent.
steps.reason: The async bot class typically mirrors the sync method’s signature and docstring; keeping parity prevents user confusion and avoids diverging generated documentation between sync and async variants.
steps.side_effects: No runtime impact; documentation-only change. Might affect async API docs/help text. Verify the updated text stays consistent with the sync version and with Telegram’s `createChatSubscriptionInviteLink` parameter semantics (e.g., units, allowed values) to avoid future support issues.
